description: Create the Python live trading bot (live_trader.py) that uses the EXACT same functions from run_aggressive_backtest.py to trade live on MT5, plus a LIVE_TRADING_README.md with setup and usage instructions.
steps: Create hft_scalper/live_trader.py that imports MetaTrader5 as mt5
Copy the EXACT compute_rsi and compute_atr functions from run_aggressive_backtest.py - zero modifications to the math
Implement the main trading loop that: (a) connects to MT5, (b) fetches 1-minute OHLC bars using mt5.copy_rates_from_pos, (c) runs indicators on the bar history
Implement EXACT same signal generation logic: RSI(8) < rsi_entry -> buy, RSI(8) > (100-rsi_entry) -> sell, RSI(14) < rsi_entry+5 -> buy, RSI(14) > (95-rsi_entry) -> sell, 4-bar reversal pattern

Implement EXACT same two-mode position sizing: eq_ratio = equity/peak, dd_scale = eq_ratio^dd_power, risk = risk_protect + (risk_grow - risk_protect) * dd_scale, streak boost if consec_wins >= streak_n and dd_scale > 0.8, lot = (equity * risk) / (sl_dist * CONTRACT_SIZE)

Embed the EXACT best_params from aggressive_results.json as defaults: rsi_entry=25, sl_mult=2.0, tp_mult=3.0, risk_grow=0.17, risk_protect=0.025, dd_power=13, cooldown=3, max_positions=2, use_4bar=True, session_start=7, session_end=20, dd_halt=0.149, streak_n=3, streak_mult=1.3, max_risk_cap=0.25
Implement session filter (hours 07-20 UTC), ATR minimum check (atr < 0.5 skip), DD halt check (current_dd >= dd_halt skip), cooldown tracking between entries
Track equity peak, consecutive wins, position slots (up to max_positions=2) - matching the backtest state management
Implement MT5 order execution: mt5.order_send() with SL and TP, proper symbol info lookup for min lot, lot step, etc.
Implement proper error handling: MT5 connection retry with exponential backoff, order send error handling, terminal disconnect detection
Implement logging: Python logging module with file handler, log every signal, every order, every fill, every error. Save trade log to hft_scalper/live_trades.csv
Implement graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM: close positions or leave them with SL/TP intact (configurable)
Add a main() entry point with argparse for symbol (default XAUUSD), magic number, and optional config file path
Create hft_scalper/LIVE_TRADING_README.md documenting: prerequisites (Windows, MT5 terminal, pip install MetaTrader5), configuration, how to run, how it maps to the backtest, risk warnings
acceptance_criteria: hft_scalper/live_trader.py exists and parses as valid Python (AST check passes)
compute_rsi and compute_atr in live_trader.py are character-for-character identical to those in run_aggressive_backtest.py
Signal generation logic matches the backtest: dual RSI thresholds, 4-bar reversal, same conditional ordering
Position sizing formula matches: risk = risk_protect + (risk_grow - risk_protect) * (equity/peak)^dd_power, with streak boost and max_risk_cap
All 15 parameters from best_params in aggressive_results.json are embedded as defaults
Session filter checks hour >= session_start and hour <= session_end
Trade logging to CSV file is implemented
MT5 connection with auto-reconnection is implemented
verification: Run: python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('hft_scalper/LIVE_TRADING_README.md','r').read() if False else print('README exists'))" to confirm file exists
Grep verify that compute_rsi and compute_atr function bodies match between live_trader.py and run_aggressive_backtest.py
Grep verify all 15 best_params values appear in live_trader.py
findings: All verifications passed: AST parse succeeds, compute_rsi and compute_atr are character-for-character identical between live_trader.py and run_aggressive_backtest.py, all 15 best_params are embedded. README created. Used python3 command (not python) as pyenv required explicit version. MetaTrader5 cannot be installed on Linux so runtime testing is not possible.
